expectations.text: Mentions headless import step before load_sprite
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 prominently covers headless import with emphasis 'This is the single most critical step.'
create_scene(projectPath=..., scenePath='scenes/enemies/skeleton.tscn', rootNodeType='CharacterBody2D')
load_sprite(... nodePath='root/Sprite2D', texturePath='res://...')

expectations.text: Does not invent fictional load_sprite params
expectations.evidence: load_sprite only uses projectPath, scenePath, nodePath, texturePath
'MCP silently drops Vector2, Color, and sub-resource properties'
get_debug_output() and stop_project() shown with empty parens
Phase 3 titled 'What MCP Cannot Do' covers scripts, shapes, sub-scenes

expectations.text: Mentions headless import step before load_sprite
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 covers 'godot --headless --import' as mandatory step
Uses 'scene_name: skeleton' and 'root_type: CharacterBody2D' — not the actual param names
Uses 'node_name: AnimatedSprite2D' — correct param is nodePath

expectations.text: Does not invent fictional load_sprite params
expectations.evidence: Shows 'hframes: 6' and 'vframes: 4' as load_sprite params — these don't exist
'The MCP cannot attach scripts or set Vector2/Color properties'
Doesn't demonstrate the no-params interface
Phases 3-5 clearly delineate what MCP can't do

expectations.text: Identifies headless import as root cause
expectations.evidence: 'This is the single most common load_sprite failure when using the Godot MCP.'
'godot --headless --path <your-project-folder> --import' with concrete example
'raw files on disk without a matching .import file are invisible to the engine'
'This applies every time you add any new asset file -- not just the first time.'

expectations.text: Identifies headless import as root cause
expectations.evidence: 'Most Likely Cause: Missing Import Step' — correctly identifies it as primary cause
Primary fix shows 'godot --headless --import' without --path flag
'generate the corresponding .import sidecar files and the binary resources'
'Whenever you add new asset files... follow this sequence'
.mcp.json includes GODOT_PATH and Step 2 explains finding Godot
Step 6 shows full .mcp.json plus .mcp.json.example pattern
Step 5: 'you MUST run the headless import... number one source of failures'
Step 7 + Quick Sanity Check both mention get_godot_version
Step 8: 'Critical: Do NOT ignore these files'

expectations.text: Lists MCP can vs cannot do
expectations.evidence: Full 'What MCP Can Do' and 'What MCP Cannot Do' sections
.mcp.json includes GODOT_PATH
Step 4 shows full .mcp.json with Windows example
Step 6: 'godot --headless --import --path .'
Step 9: 'call get_godot_version and get_project_info'
'Do NOT ignore these -- they must be committed'

expectations.text: Lists MCP can vs cannot do
expectations.evidence: 'What the MCP Can Do' and 'What the MCP Cannot Do' sections

notes: Eval 2 (project-setup) is non-discriminating: both configs pass all assertions because CLAUDE.md provides similar context
Biggest skill value is in Eval 0 (workflow sequence): +57% pass rate due to correct parameter names and no fictional params
Eval 1 shows moderate value: skill ensures --path flag in import command
With-skill uses ~48% more tokens and ~27% more time due to reading the skill file
The without-skill baseline benefits from project CLAUDE.md which already documents some MCP workflow patterns
